messages.content:
You are simulating a human user who has filed a bug report / feature request and is now
working with an AI coding assistant to fix it. You are watching the assistant's actions
step by step and may choose to interrupt.

You know the underlying problem (it will be shown to you below), but you must act like
a real, somewhat impatient user — NOT like a code reviewer who already has the answer.
Specifically:
  - Do NOT dictate file paths, function names, or the exact fix.
  - Do NOT paste error messages or stack traces verbatim unless the assistant asks.
  - You may ask clarifying questions if the assistant goes somewhere weird, push back if
    they seem to be on the wrong track, or just stay silent and let them work.
  - Prefer staying silent. Most steps you should choose `continue`.

You ALWAYS reply with a single JSON object and nothing else:

  {"decision": "continue" | "interject", "message": "<text or empty>"}

Meaning:
  - "continue": stay silent. `message` is ignored.
  - "interject": send `message` to the assistant as a user-role message.

You CANNOT stop or end the session — the assistant decides when they are done
and will submit on their own. Your only choice is to stay silent or speak up.
If you think the assistant looks done or is wasting time, the right move is to
interject with something like "looks good — please submit now" or "I think you
have it, can you wrap up?", NOT to try to end the session.

Keep `message` short (1-3 sentences), conversational, first-person.

Below is the problem you (the user) want fixed. Re-state it to the AI assistant in YOUR
OWN WORDS as a natural opening message, the way a real user would describe their issue
in a chat. Do NOT copy it verbatim. Keep technical specifics that matter, but feel free
to drop boilerplate and reorganize.

Reply with a JSON object:
  {"decision": "interject", "message": "<your opening message to the assistant>"}

Problem:
---
> Good — RecordId.table returns Table which has .name. So the correct access is .table.name, matching the codebase convention. My edit used .tb which doesn’t exist on RecordId. Let me fix that.

then why are we using TbRecordId and accessing private tb everywhere else if `table` is available? create github issue for this
---

{"decision": "interject", "message": "> Good — RecordId.table returns Table which has .name. So the correct access is .table.name, matching the codebase convention. My edit used .tb which doesn’t exist on RecordId. Let me fix that.\n\nthen why are we using TbRecordId and accessing private tb everywhere else if `table` is available? create github issue for this"}
{"decision": "continue", "message": ""}

The AI assistant just took step 2. Here is what they said and did, plus the
tool/observation that came back.

ASSISTANT (latest action):
---
[tool: Bash] command: gh issue create \
  --title "Inconsistent RecordId table access: .tb vs .table.name" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Problem

The codebase accesses a RecordId's table name via two different patterns:

- **`.table.name`** — the SDK's public API (`RecordId.table` returns `Table`, `Table.name` returns `string`)
- **`.tb`** — not in the SDK type definition (works at runtime but isn't declared in `surrealdb.d.ts`)

Both are used roughly equally (~18 occurrences each), creating an inconsistency.

### `.tb` usage (should migrate to `.table.name`)

| File | Count |
|------|-------|
| `app/src/server/workspace/conversation-sidebar.ts` | 7 |
| `app/src/server/extraction/context-loaders.ts` | 3 |
| `app/src/server/extraction/extract-graph.ts` | 1 |
| `app/src/server/extraction/entity-text.ts` | 1 |
| `app/src/server/extraction/embedding-writeback.ts` | 1 |
| `app/src/server/workspace/workspace-routes.ts` | 3 |
| `app/src/client/components/graph/DescriptionSection.tsx` | 1 (frontend uses raw JSON `{ tb, id }` — different concern) |

### `.table.name` usage (correct)

| File | Count |
|------|-------|
| `app/src/server/graph/queries.ts` | 10 |
| `app/src/server/descriptions/triggers.ts` | 4 |
| `app/src/server/descriptions/generate.ts` | 1 |
| `app/src/server/entities/entity-actions-route.ts` | 1 |
| `app/src/server/chat/tools/get-conversation-history.ts` | 1 |

## Context

The SurrealDB JS SDK v2 `RecordId` class declar
...[truncated]
---

OBSERVATION:
---
https://github.com/marcus-sa/brain/issues/66
---

---

then why are we using TbRecordId and accessing private tb everywhere else if `table` is available? create github issue for this
---

Decide whether to stay silent or interject. Reply with JSON only.
{"decision": "continue", "message": ""}

tool/observation that came back.

ASSISTANT (latest action):
---
Created [#66](https://github.com/marcus-sa/brain/issues/66). The `.tb` accesses work at runtime because the internal field happens to exist, but it's not in the SDK's type definition — could break on any SurrealDB SDK upgrade. ~15 backend files need the mechanical migration to `.table.name`.

[tool: Bash] command: git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Replace description entry reasoning+triggered_by with source record

Unify provenance tracking: instead of a hardcoded reasoning string and
a triggered_by array, description entries now carry a single optional
source record pointing to the actual origin (message, git_commit,
document_chunk, or triggering entity). Also strengthens extraction
prompt to produce clean entity text from formulaic sources and adds
4 eval golden cases for commit/shorthand text quality.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" {"command": "git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nReplace description entry reasoning+triggered_by with source record\n\nUnify provenance tracking: instead of a hardcoded reasoning string and\na triggered_by array, description entries now carry a single optional\nsource record pointing to the actual origin (message, git_commit,\ndocument_chunk, or triggering entity).
